topic: A coach has to cut a player from the team
raw_output:
[story 1]

The fluorescent lights of the locker room hummed, a high, irritating whine that seemed to vibrate directly in my chest. Coach Miller stood by the whiteboard, his shadow looming large across the polished floor. Every breath I took felt shallow, catching in my throat. I kept my eyes fixed on the scuff marks near my worn sneakers, trying to make myself small, invisible. When he finally spoke, his voice was level, devoid of warmth, which somehow made the pronouncement feel heavier than any shouting could have been. "This roster needs adjustments," he stated, tapping a red marker against a name. My hands started to tremble, a faint tremor I couldn't suppress, and I felt a sudden, cold tightening around my stomach. I just nodded, unable to meet his gaze, the silence stretching out until it felt like an eternity, waiting for the inevitable next blow.

[story 2]

My palms were slick against the laminated clipboard, the paper threatening to slip from my grasp. The air in the gymnasium felt thick, suffocating, as if the very oxygen was being rationed just for me. Coach Vance approached, his expression unreadable, and the way he adjusted his collar made my skin crawl. I swallowed hard, the dryness in my mouth making the action painfully audible. When he finally delivered the news—that I wouldn't be making the final cut—a strange numbness washed over me, followed by a dizzying sense of unreality. I could feel my legs locking up; every muscle in my body seemed to seize. I wanted to bolt, to disappear into the bleachers, but my feet felt rooted to the spot, unable to move, paralyzed by the sheer weight of the decision hanging in the air between us.

[story 3]

I watched the coach’s face, cataloging every subtle shift in his expression—the tightening around his jaw, the slight narrowing of his eyes. My heart hammered against my ribs, a frantic, uneven rhythm that echoed loudly in the sudden quiet of the team meeting. When he finally called my name, the sound seemed distant, muffled. I tried to gather my composure, to project confidence, but my voice came out thin and reedy. The moment he mentioned my name in the context of the cuts, a wave of intense physical discomfort washed over me. I squeezed my eyes shut for a brief second, fighting the urge to look down at the floor, trying desperately to regulate the frantic energy surging beneath my skin before I could lose control entirely.
